examples: This seemed to exasperate the Prince.
R: young man, do not exasperate a man who neglects the dead!
To Enrage or exasperate someone is to write an abusive personal attack.
Forty-eight teams are a lot--so many that they would exasperate the attention span of even ardent fans.
Hunt and destroy algorithms may exasperate the situation by taking out the stops, thereby driving Apple artificially lower.
